sections.paragraph:
προσβλέπω fut. προσβλέψω (Ps 83:10 Sym.); 1 aor. impv. 2 sg. πρόσβλεψον (Jon 2:5 Sym., Theod.)

① to fix one’s gaze upon, gaze at, look at (X., Symp. 3, 14; Plut., Cato Min. 791 [65, 11]; Lucian, Alex. 42, Dial. Mer. 11, 4; Philo, Abr. 76. In earlier Gk. [Soph. et al.] in this sense usually w. the acc., so also Philo, Op. M. 152; Just., D. 94, 3.) look at τοῖς ἁγίοις σκεύεσιν Ox 840, 29.

② to consider from a special point of view, look upon, regard, w. the acc. (Aeschyl., Pla., X. et al.; Vett. Val. 114, 25; SIG 1168, 44) τὸν ἐπίσκοπον ὡς αὐτὸν τὸν κύριον προσβλέπειν regard the overseer as the Lord himself IEph 6:1.

fut. fut. = fut.

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. sg. = singular

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

acc. acc. = accusative

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 876
